mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, which compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to predict margin compression proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising housing starts signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and HOUST shows no predictive power for SPX 5d returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising consumer credit balances signal sustained demand that supports corporate earnings multiples, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in late-cycle periods do not significantly outperform baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or RSAFS growth decouples from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 240, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting fears of aggressive monetary tightening that compress equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses 240, or the signal fails to predict volatility spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes against SPX 60d returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts signals manufacturing-led demand recovery rather than real estate speculation, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate > HOUST growth rate (quarterly)
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and HOUST growth rates shows no correlation with SPX 20d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or the signal reverses direction in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5%, rising unemployment acts as a recession warning that triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and UNRATE rising > 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative when both FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and UNRATE rises, or the joint signal fails to predict drawdowns in walk-forward testing.
